title: Skopeo should support for BigFilesTemporaryDir (SystemContext)
body:
Enhancement request: https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/805
Tested locally and works, but as this is my first time contributing to this project I am not entirely sure about style/code practices around here.

comments.body:
> Buildah and Podman man defaults to $TMPDIR which is set to /var/tmp by default.

That’s not how $TMPDIR is defined to work; it usually points to a `/tmp`-like directory (which on systemd Linuxes tends to be tmpfs, thus undesirable for this purpose). Compare https://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/xrat/V4_xbd_chap10.html and https://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/utilities/V3_chap01.html .

(To clarify, the “which is set to `/var/tmp` by default” means “Podman/Buildah set it that way”, not “Linux systems set it that way by default”).

Given the systemd re-definition of `/tmp` (which… happened), using TMPDIR for the “big files” directory overloads the variable with no way for the user to set a consistent value for container tools and the others.

> I think this option should be on `skopeo copy`, not on `skopeo`

No, consider horrible things like `skopeo inspect docker-daemon:…`, which use a `docker save`, save the (potentially huge!) result into a temporary file, and then read that temporary file to find the relevant parts of the archive.

A global option is appropriate.
I reformatted the code changes and it passes checks.

I hate multi work with multi "-" options.  I think they are for people who type better then I do.  
Please just use --tmpdir and explain that it is for storing big temporary files.  Have it defaulted to /var/tmp

> Have it defaulted to /var/tmp

Not that part. c/image already defaults to `/var/tmp` if the `BigFilesTemporaryDir` override is not present, we shouldn’t be maintaining the hard-coded value in two places.
@mtrmac @malaiwah What is going on with this PR?
@rhatdan Have the review comments been addressed?
If they haven't we should be encouraging @malaiwah to finish the PR. Not letting it languish.
:-) That is on my list.. true... true..
